senses.gloss: a kind of small skiff with a curved bow, sambuq

etymology.text:
From Middle Persian *sanbūk whence Classical Persian سنبک (sumbuk), from Sanskrit शम्बूक (śambūka, literally “snail-shell”), or less likely Malay sampan, from Chinese 舢板 (MC paenX).
To be kept apart from the name of the xebec / sciabecco type of vessel which was used in the Mediterranean and is derived only in the 15ᵗʰ century CE and later from Arabic شُبَّاك (šubbāk).
